detaileddescription.para: Constructs an empty (default-constructed) instance. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_optional_std_variant_MR_Sphere3f_MR_Features_Primitives_ConeSegment_MR_Features_Primitives_Plane_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Constructs an array of empty (default-constructed) instances, of the specified size. Will never return null. The array must be destroyed using MR_std_optional_std_variant_MR_Sphere3f_MR_Features_Primitives_ConeSegment_MR_Features_Primitives_Plane_DestroyArray(). Use MR_std_optional_std_variant_MR_Sphere3f_MR_Features_Primitives_ConeSegment_MR_Features_Primitives_Plane_OffsetMutablePtr() and MR_std_optional_std_variant_MR_Sphere3f_MR_Features_Primitives_ConeSegment_MR_Features_Primitives_Plane_OffsetPtr() to access the array elements.

Constructs a copy of another instance. The source remains alive. Parameter other can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to the parameter other might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_optional_std_variant_MR_Sphere3f_MR_Features_Primitives_ConeSegment_MR_Features_Primitives_Plane_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it held previously.

Constructs a new instance. Parameter other is a single object. Parameter other is optional. To keep it empty, pass a null pointer. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_optional_std_variant_MR_Sphere3f_MR_Features_Primitives_ConeSegment_MR_Features_Primitives_Plane_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Assigns the contents from another instance. Both objects remain alive after the call. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter other can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to the parameter other might be preserved in this object. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it held previously.
Assigns the contents. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter other is a single object. Parameter other is optional. To keep it empty, pass a null pointer.

briefdescription.para: Destroys a heap-allocated instance of MR_std_optional_std_variant_MR_Sphere3f_MR_Features_Primitives_ConeSegment_MR_Features_Primitives_Plane. Does nothing if the pointer is null.
Destroys a heap-allocated array of MR_std_optional_std_variant_MR_Sphere3f_MR_Features_Primitives_ConeSegment_MR_Features_Primitives_Plane. Does nothing if the pointer is null.

detaileddescription.para: Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.
Returns true if this instance stores an object, as opposed to being empty. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
The stored element or null if none, read-only. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.
The stored element or null if none, mutable. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.
